question:
def select_words(s, n):
    """Given a string s and a natural number n, you have been tasked to implement 
    a function that returns a list of all words from string s that contain exactly 
    n consonants, in order these words appear in the string s.
    If the string s is empty then the function should return an empty list.
    Note: you may assume the input string contains only letters and spaces.
    Examples:
    select_words("Mary had a little lamb", 4) ==> ["little"]
    select_words("Mary had a little lamb", 3) ==> ["Mary", "lamb"]
    select_words("simple white space", 2) ==> []
    select_words("Hello world", 4) ==> ["world"]
    select_words("Uncle sam", 3) ==> ["Uncle"]
    """

trajectory.content:
1. A whimsical wordsmith who can weave tales from the fabric of language. Known for their ability to turn mundane sentences into lyrical poetry, they once wrote a sonnet to a sandwich that became a bestseller in the culinary world. Their knack for identifying consonants in the most unexpected places will surely help in selecting the right words.

2. A quirky mathematician who believes that numbers have personalities. They once hosted a dinner party where each guest was assigned a number and had to act out its characteristics. Their unique perspective on counting consonants will bring a fresh approach to the problem, ensuring that every word is counted with flair and precision. 

3. A retired pirate who has taken to the seas of literature. With a parrot named "Syntax" perched on their shoulder, they navigate the treacherous waters of grammar and punctuation. Their tales of treasure hunts for the perfect word will inspire the team to dig deep into the string of letters and uncover the gems hidden within.

4. A mystical librarian who guards the secrets of ancient texts. With a library that stretches into infinity, they have read every book ever written and can recall the exact number of consonants in any word. Their wisdom will guide the team in understanding the nuances of language and the importance of each consonant in the quest for the perfect list of words.

5. A whimsical chef who specializes in creating dishes inspired by literature. They once made a soufflé that rose to the occasion of a Shakespearean play, and their culinary creations often reflect the rhythm and flow of language. Their creative approach to combining flavors will inspire the team to blend logic and creativity in their coding efforts.
